conflicts.risk: Planning can become too verbose for tasks that should stay lean.
conflicts.resolution: Plan before non-trivial work, then prune the plan to the smallest shape that still reduces risk and supports verification.

conflicts.risk: A visible requirements ledger can add verbosity while caveman-ultra demands terse output.
conflicts.resolution: Track requirements in the ledger or task system as needed, while keeping user-facing prose terse when caveman-ultra is active.

conflicts.risk: Memory writes can preserve unverified or low-signal facts.
conflicts.resolution: write-gate controls persistence; wiki-memory writes only verified, durable, useful facts that pass the gate.

conflicts.risk: Loop design can become process-heavy or add unnecessary delegation.
conflicts.resolution: Use loop-engineering only for real generator-verifier or retry loops, and cap scope, budget, and stop conditions up front.

conflicts.risk: A task retrospective can start before evidence exists, or verification can imply an automatic learning write.
conflicts.resolution: Run fresh verification first; only armed task-retrospective or explicit persistence requests may route durable project lessons through write-gate.

conflicts.risk: Model-routing hooks can save tokens while also changing hot-path cache behavior.
conflicts.resolution: Keep prompt-triage routing explicit in hook config and run cache-lint before relying on prompt-cache measurements.

conflicts.risk: Both use audit language; an agent could confuse Brainer skill-use audit with project task learning.
conflicts.resolution: Default 'activate audit mode' to brainer-audit unless the user uses task/project-learning language. task-retrospective writes only project learnings; brainer-audit is report-only by default and proposes Brainer improvements.
